other_observers: Bill Schmoker photographed the bird, Larry Semo, Rachel Hopper, Tony Leukering, Mark Peterson, Bill Maynard, Joey Kellner, Ric Olson, Dick Schottler, Joe Himmel, etc.; Not the original finder - found by friend of Tony Leukering.
specific_location: Bear Creek Lake in Bear Creek County Park.
description: Small alcid, very much smaller than nearby Gadwalls. Yellow bill, dark eye, black face and nape. A white patch extended from behind the eye onto the lower face and the breast. The back was a uniform dark gray, as was the inner half of the wings - the outer wings were black. The body near the waterline and the under tail was a dirty gray. Did not see the underwings or breast because the bird did not fly. The chin was dark, but was a smudge rather than uniform. Sibley shows the adult with a dark chin, and the juvenile with a rather light gray smudge. Thus, I am not sure if this was a juvenile, more likely, or an adult.Behaviors: Sitting on a small lake, and occasionally flapped wings. Did not fly or dive.Call: No sounds heard.Plumage: Basic winter
